summary: The closest documented direct analogue to the post-2025 fact pattern. SEC filings establish the financial interest; NYT and FT reporting establish the PIF source. Pre-2025 timing places it in the comparative set rather than the live tracker.

First-term hospitality-revenue fact pattern. CREW reporting, House Oversight Committee documents, and the underlying foreign Emoluments litigation established the records. Comparative for methodology calibration on what counts as a foreign government official acting in official capacity payment.

Tests the state-grant-as-payment framing. Trademarks were awarded by a Chinese state IP office on a non-routine timeline coinciding with her White House role. Comparative reference for whether non-cash sovereign benefits register as foreign Emoluments Clause flows.

Anchor case for the foreign Emoluments Clause modern doctrinal posture. Reached the Fourth Circuit on standing and zone-of-interests; vacated and remanded as moot in January 2021 after Trump left office. The case law on emolument definition still governs the framework this tracker uses.
DC Circuit held individual members of Congress lacked standing to sue over foreign Emoluments Clause violations absent institutional authorization. Load-bearing for any future enforcement challenge.

Documented for methodology completeness as the case the framework excludes despite political salience. Burisma is a Cyprus-registered private holding company; no Ukrainian state ownership in the documented record. State ties run through the owner's prior ministerial tenure, not through ownership structure. The exclusion is symmetrical: a Republican-side analogue with the same facts would also fall outside scope.
documented_amount: ~$3.4M to Rosemont Seneca Bohai LLC (entity-level inflows)

summary: Decentralized finance protocol and stablecoin issuer founded 2024 by Zachary Folkman, Chase Herro, the Witkoff family (Alex and Zach Witkoff), and Trump family members (Donald Trump Jr., Eric Trump, Barron Trump). Donald Trump and Steve Witkoff are listed in WLF corporate documentation as co-founders emeritus. Trump family entities receive 75% of net WLFI token-sale proceeds plus a cut of stablecoin profits. Three sovereign-adjacent transactions sit on the record. On January 16, 2025, four days before inauguration, Aryam (a vehicle identified in WSJ reporting as controlled by lieutenants of Sheikh Tahnoon bin Zayed Al Nahyan, UAE National Security Advisor and chair of ADQ, IHC, G42, and MGX) acquired 49% of WLF for $500M, with $187M flowing to Trump family entities and $31M flowing to Witkoff-associated entities at signing. The transaction was not publicly disclosed for over a year. On May 1, 2025, MGX (Abu Dhabi state-backed, Tahnoon chair) used WLF's USD1 stablecoin to settle a $2B Binance investment. On January 14, 2026, the Pakistan Virtual Asset Regulatory Authority (PVARA) and the Pakistan Ministry of Finance signed a non-binding MOU with SC Financial Technologies (a Delaware-registered WLF affiliate that co-owns the USD1 stablecoin brand) to explore integrating USD1 into Pakistan's regulated cross-border payments framework. Pakistani Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb signed for Pakistan; Zach Witkoff (CEO of both WLF and SC Financial Technologies) signed for the US side. The signing ceremony was attended by Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Field Marshal Asim Munir. The MOU is non-binding and exploratory; no Pakistani-sovereign payment to SC Financial Technologies or WLF is documented as of this entry. The Tahnoon-Aryam transaction carries the v2.0 convergent-interest flag for Witkoff Group and WLF portfolio overlap; Steve Witkoff served as Middle East envoy from early 2025, with continuing equity exposures across Witkoff Group affiliates and WLF-related vehicles per his August 13, 2025 OGE Form 278e (New Entrant Report). Pre-v2.0 records are not re-examined; this is the worked v1.x case the v2.0 flag was developed against.
documented_amount: $500M Aryam stake (Tahnoon, Jan 2025); $2B MGX-Binance via USD1 (May 2025)

family_member: Steve Witkoff (Special Envoy for the Middle East; covered intermediary under v2.0)
summary: v2.0 covered-intermediary worked case. On February 19, 2026, the Pakistani Finance Ministry and the US General Services Administration signed a non-binding MOU to jointly redevelop the Roosevelt Hotel, a 22-story 1,025-room property in midtown Manhattan owned by Pakistan International Airlines. Per the Pakistani government statement, the agreement was 'negotiated and stewarded' by US Special Envoy for the Middle East Steve Witkoff. Pakistani officials estimate the asset value at over $1 billion. Under the proposed structure, Pakistan contributes the land while a development partner puts up roughly $1B equity and the joint venture takes on $2-3B in debt. PIA was privatized in December 2025 as part of Pakistan's IMF Extended Fund Facility restructuring; the Pakistani government retains a minority stake. Witkoff is the founder of Witkoff Group, a New York real estate firm whose Manhattan portfolio includes the Times Square Edition and the Faena, both luxury redevelopment projects in the same market segment as the Roosevelt. NY Post and Reuters reporting describes Witkoff as having potential post-diplomatic interest in Roosevelt-related deals; this is reporting speculation, not a contractual position. Witkoff Group is not a party to the MOU, and as of this entry no PIA-source flow to Witkoff Group is documented. The convergent interest is at the role-and-portfolio level: covered intermediary (envoy with foreign-policy portfolio overlapping the deal counterparty), Manhattan real estate firm whose sector aligns with the redevelopment plan, sovereign-adjacent counterparty (post-privatization PIA with Pakistani government minority stake). Witkoff's continuing equity exposures across Witkoff Group affiliates are documented on the August 13, 2025 OGE Form 278e referenced in SC-007. The record will be updated if Witkoff Group becomes a contractual party or a PIA-source flow is documented.
documented_amount: $1B+ asset value (estimated by Pakistan); no documented Witkoff Group receipt as of February 2026
